mentions.note: AI chip winner with staying power; strong performer in 2025.
Bid tone as TSM fortune lifts semiconductor capital equipment and fabless design ecosystem; beneficiary of insatiable AI chip demand.
Cramer likes AMD over Micron as potential pullback entry; beneficiary of memory chip shortage demand; better entry opportunity on any dip.
Rallied nicely as they DID factor shortage into guidance; wish we hadn't sold for Charitable Trust. Beneficiary of Intel's execution stumble.
Pattern has emerged where terrific numbers still result in stock decline; semiconductor sector headwind despite fundamental strength.

Down 3.5% today despite bearish narrative that AMD nipping at Nvidia's heels. Program selling affected all hardware indiscriminately; AMD down despite no negative company news. Treat as program-driven opportunity, not fundamental collapse.
Subject to same export gate rumors as Nvidia; would hurt addressable market and competitive position if restrictions applied.
Semiconductor upswing; killing it on hardware-centric AI infrastructure buildout.
CPU and GPU maker; up 42% since bottom, #2 CPU producer for AI data centers. Monolithic Power Systems co-benefits (+47%). Parabolic move; Cramer cautions all these semis are too hot now.
CPU competitor to Nvidia for agentic AI infrastructure; persistent institutional demand; Cramer should be wearing post-its.
Competitive threat to high-flying semiconductor longs; may disrupt current winners.
CPU complex will do well rest of year. Up 70% since March 30; in rest-and-relaxation mode after 37% April gain.
Parabolic move; wish trust owned it; healthy correction risk.
Lisa Su is the best. Cramer's biggest upside surprise bet for the week; data-center tailwind continues.
CPU shortage from AI agent workloads is structural; today's analyst peak call is wrong — shortage hasn't ended so estimates remain too low; use downgrades to buy.
Fantastic quarter; Lisa Su appearing on Walk the Street; beneficiary of AI and data center chip demand.
CPU market growth accelerating to 35% YoY from 18% expected; analyst downgrade proved dead wrong on earnings beat; up 18% today.
Trades at 21x sales vs. Cerebras 187x — far cheaper. Profitable business at reasonable multiples for strong growth story.
Semiconductor cohort powering AI; mentioned as important but Nvidia is primary play in semis sector.
Fantastic company at $500/share; both AMD and NVIDIA are great, but NVIDIA is Cramer's favorite right now; both good buys.
